etymology.description: 白(白) (bái, "white") + 夲(夲) (tǎo, "to advance") = unclear connection to modern meaning -> final form 皋
etymology.interpretation: The character 皋 combines 白(白) (bái, "white") with 夲(夲) (tǎo, "to advance") in an ideogrammic fashion, although the reasoning behind how these elements contribute to the meaning 'marsh' is not well understood.

examples.chinese: 这种植物常见于湿润的皋地(這種植物常見於濕潤的皋地)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng zhíwù chángjiàn yú shīrùn de gāo dì.
examples.english: This kind of plant is commonly found in marshy areas.

examples.chinese: 河流两岸的皋地(河流兩岸的皋地)孕育了丰富的生态系统(孕育了豐富的生態系統)。
examples.pinyin: Héliú liǎng'àn de gāo dì yùnyùle fēngfù de shēngtài xìtǒng.
examples.english: The marshlands on both sides of the river have nurtured a rich ecosystem.

examples.chinese: 古代诗人常常在皋地(古代詩人常常在皋地)写下优美的诗句(寫下優美的詩句)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài shīrén chángcháng zài gāo dì xiěxià yōuměi de shījù.
examples.english: Ancient poets often wrote beautiful verses in the marshlands.
